framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link -- the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Building and publicly maintaining free software packages (in R, Python, etc.) that implement core actuarial methods -- claims reserving, loss-distribution fitting, life and pension projection models -- so the whole profession can use and inspect them.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you combine deep technical command of the methods with the software discipline to make that command usable and durable for others — a rarer pairing than passing exams alone.

paths.what_it_is: Producing a public, citable body of original technical work -- papers, monographs, or textbooks -- that applies statistical learning and data-science methods to core actuarial problems like pricing, reserving, and claims.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can push the discipline's methods forward rather than only apply the existing toolkit — judgment an exam transcript alone cannot show.

paths.path: Public actuarial teaching and exam-prep content
paths.what_it_is: Writing, coaching, or publishing books and courses that teach other candidates how to pass actuarial exams and build a career, done publicly and by name while working as a credentialed actuary.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves communication skill and initiative beyond passing exams privately -- the same qualities that make someone worth trusting to represent an employer to clients and regulators.
